rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aryl-/alkyl-sulfonylation or alkoxysulfonylation of N-tert-butyl N-allylbenzamides coupled with a Truce–Smiles rearrangement to access β-arylethylamine sulfones, sulfonate esters, and sulfonic acids.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates the sulfonyl radical (from arylsulfonyl hydrazide via SET/deprotonation/N2 loss) or SO2 radical cation (from NaHSO3 via SO2 release and SET); after radical addition and Truce–Smiles rearrangement, the carbamoyl radical is further oxidized at the anode to a cation trapped by methanol, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: sulfonyl radical Ia (ArSO2• or MeSO2•) from anodic oxidation/N2 extrusion of sulfonyl hydrazide
SO2 radical cation Ib generated from NaHSO3 anodic oxidation
γ-sulfonyl β-amino C-centered radical IIa (from sulfonyl radical addition to alkene)
γ-(methoxysulfonyl) β-amino alkyl radical IIb (from SO2/MeOH trapping of alkene)
spirocyclic cyclohexadienyl radical III (5-exo-trig ipso-cyclization intermediate of Truce–Smiles)
carbamoyl N-centered radical IV after C–C bond cleavage / aryl migration
carbamoyl/acylium-type cation V formed by anodic oxidation of IV

electron_flow: At the anode, the sulfonyl hydrazide (path a) or NaHSO3 (path b) is oxidized to generate a sulfonyl radical (Ia) or SO2 radical cation (Ib), which adds to the allyl alkene to give a β-amino C-radical II; intramolecular ipso-addition onto the N-aryl group (5-exo-trig) yields spirocyclic radical III, which fragments to a carbamoyl radical IV. IV is oxidized at the anode to cation V, trapped by methanol to deliver the methyl carbamate product, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a, sulfonyl hydrazide 2 and NaHSO3
probe_reagents.observation: Ep/2(1a)=+1.49 V, (4-F-C6H4)SO2NHNH2 = +0.93 V, NaHSO3 = +0.67 V vs Fc+/0 in CH3CN/MeOH; sulfur components are oxidized first, supporting SET initiation at the sulfur reagent rather than the amide.

probe_reagents.name: current off-control (Table 1, entry 15)
probe_reagents.observation: Without applied current, no product forms, confirming the essential role of electrolysis.

notes: A competing ortho-addition pathway of the alkyl radical onto the N-aryl ring (instead of ipso-substitution) was observed with the 2-thiophenyl substrate 1o, giving dihydrothienopyridinone 4n' (46%) alongside the expected Truce–Smiles product 4n (24%). Two parallel mechanistic pathways are proposed: path a (aryl-/alkyl-sulfonylation from ArSO2NHNH2) and path b (alkoxysulfonylation from NaHSO3 + MeOH); both converge at the radical Truce–Smiles step.
